zh.lifeSummary: 當Charles II d'ALBRET於從1397年到1407年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛內拉可時，他的父親Charles I d'ALBRET46歲，母親Marie de Sully48歲。他於1417年與Anne D’Armagnac在法國布列塔尼結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1471年在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Charles II d'ALBRET于从1397年到1407年出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛內拉可时，他的父亲Charles I d'ALBRET46岁，母亲Marie de Sully48岁。他于1417年与Anne D’Armagnac在法国布列塔尼结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1471年在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Charles II d'ALBRET was born from 1397 to 1407, in Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, his father, Charles I d'ALBRET, was 46 and his mother, Marie de Sully, was 48. He married Anne D’Armagnac in 1417, in Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1471, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Charles II d'ALBRET est né du 1397 au 1407, à Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Charles I d'ALBRET, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Marie de Sully, avait 48 ans. Il a épousé Anne D’Armagnac en 1417, à Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1471, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Charles II d'ALBRET von 1397 bis 1407 in Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Charles I d'ALBRET 46 und seine Mutter Marie de Sully 48. Er hat 1417 in Bretagne, Frankreich Anne D’Armagnac geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1471 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Charles II d'ALBRET è nato il da 1397 a 1407 nel Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, il suo padre, Charles I d'ALBRET, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Marie de Sully, aveva 48 anni. Lui sposato Anne D’Armagnac nel 1417, nel Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1471, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles II d'ALBRETがから1397年まで1407年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインロト-エト-ガーオンンeラックで生まれた時，父Charles I d'ALBRETは46歳，母Marie de Sullyは48歳であった。1417年にフランスブレタグンでAnne D’Armagnacと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1471年に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles II d'ALBRET(이)가 1397년 부터 1407년 까지 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 로트-트-가니 e라크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Charles I d'ALBRET(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie de Sully(은)는 48세였다. 그는 1417년 프랑스 브레타그니에서 Anne D’Armagnac(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1471년 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Charles II d'ALBRET nasceu de 1397 a 1407, em Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Charles I d'ALBRET, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Marie de Sully, tinha 48 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne D’Armagnac em 1417, em Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1471, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Charles II d'ALBRET родился с 1397 по 1407 в местности нeрак, лот-ет-гаронн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, его отцу по имени Charles I d'ALBRET было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie de Sully, 48. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne D’Armagnac в 1417 г. в местности бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1471 г. в его родном городе.

sm.BET 1397 AND 1407–1471: mai le 1397 e o'o I le 1407–1471
sm.BET 1397 AND 1407: mai le 1397 e o'o I le 1407

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Charles II d'ALBRET nació desde 1397 hasta 1407, en Nérac, Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Charles I d'ALBRET, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Marie de Sully, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Anne D’Armagnac en 1417, en Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1471, en su pueblo.
